steps.title: Find the radius slope
steps.content:
The radius goes from the center $(-2,2)$ to the point of tangency $(6,-4)$. Its slope is
$$\frac{-4-2}{6-(-2)}=\frac{-6}{8}=-\frac{3}{4}$$

steps.title: Write the tangent line
steps.content:
A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius, so its slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$\frac{4}{3}$$
Using point $(6,-4)$:
$$y+4=\frac{4}{3}(x-6)$$
$$y=\frac{4}{3}x-12$$

steps.title: Test the listed points
steps.content: For $x=12$, the line gives $$y=\frac{4}{3}(12)-12=16-12=4$$ so $(12,4)$ lies on the tangent line. The answer is C.
